1.0.1.en: Take Homey's TZ into consideration in the UI

1.0.2.en: Fixed devices not restoring during capacity shortfall. Burst rate now only limited in last 10 minutes of the hour. Added dry-run warning banner. Added end-of-hour indicator in plan view.

1.0.3.en: Improved settings UI with better spacing and navigation. Added stale data warning banner. Improved price display with collapsible summary. Renamed insights driver with new icon.

1.0.4.en: Fix shortfall detection during end of hour

1.0.5.en: Renaming modes should now be safe

1.1.0.en: Added new card for when operating mode changes

1.1.1.en: Improved flow card UX: removed parentheses from report power card title and added helpful usage hints. All flow cards now provide clear guidance on how to use them with practical examples.

1.3.0.en: New flow condition: “Headroom for device” to check if a device can safely increase its consumption by X kW.

1.3.1.en: More robust headroom calculation for device

1.4.0.en: Upgrade the plan UI with more structured details

1.5.0.en: Added configurable overshoot shedding per device (turn off vs minimum temperature), plus updated UI and docs to support it.

1.5.2.en: Gate sheds and swaps on measurements

1.5.3.en: Coordinated price optimization and capacity planning, plus various bug‑fix tests and safeguards.

1.5.4.en: Improved plan behavior and clarity: plan status reasons simplified, shed temps validated on repeated overshoot, and power estimation unified for restore logic.

1.5.5.en: Large refactor of the codebase and various bugfixes.

1.5.6.en: Fix crash due to excessive CPU usage and converted more internals to use UTC.

1.5.8.en: Improved settings UI power usage visuals and labels, now using Homey’s timezone. Settings UI errors are logged to Homey. Plan view usage and clarity improvements.

1.5.9.en: Update app descriptions and doc fixes.

1.6.0.en: Added a soft daily energy budget feature with price‑shaped planning and limiter status, including a new “today plan” view and daily budget settings, while refining plan logic (freeze/settle behavior) and performance; refreshed Insights (limit reason/load split, removed deprecated capabilities), reorganized the settings UI, and introduced topic‑based debug logging and timezone handling fixes alongside assorted plan/UX cleanup.

1.7.0.en: Added new flow actions to enable and disable capacity control per device, giving you more flexibility to manage which devices participate in load shedding through automations. Fixed an issue where daily budget constraints could incorrectly trigger shortfall alerts - daily budget limits are now treated as soft constraints that won't cause emergency shedding. Improved the settings UI with a usage legend and better device control visibility.

1.7.1.en: Information previously only available when hovering over e.g chart bars is now available on touch interfaces as well.

1.7.2.en:
Previously the 'Used X of Y kWh' line always showed the hourly capacity
budget, even when the system was constrained by daily budget. Now the UI shows the actual daily budget allocation for the current hour.

1.7.3.en: The Daily Budget panel has been refreshed with a today/tomorrow toggle, a clearer planned‑vs‑actual chart plus confidence chip, and a simplified summary that includes remaining/deviation and an estimated cost readout (now shown in NOK); tomorrow’s plan preview appears when prices are available; nettleie (grid tariff) fetching is more reliable thanks to fallback dates when the NVE API returns empty data, so price totals are less likely to go missing.

1.7.4.en: Added a new Flow action to set or disable the daily budget (kWh) so you can automate budget changes, and the settings UI now arranges tabs into two rows for better navigation.

1.7.6.en: PELS now reduces restore flapping by adding an exponential restore cooldown backoff (up to 5 minutes) after instability, improves shedding order so lower‑priority devices shed first even if higher‑priority devices were recently restored, makes daily budget deviation time‑weighted within the hour for more accurate “allowed so far” reporting, and simplifies device status messaging to show “keep” without priority while noting when a device was recently restored.

1.8.0.en: App’s price calculations and UI have been expanded to show a full hourly price breakdown: totals now include grid tariff energy component, provider surcharge (now defined as VAT‑inclusive), consumption tax, Enova fee, VAT, and electricity support (with regional rules like NO4 VAT exemption and reduced consumption tax in Troms/Finnmark), and the Price tab reflects this with clearer labels and hover tooltips that show the component breakdown per hour.

1.8.1.en: Add debug tools for cleaning up device states and device insights.

1.8.3.en: Added support for socket on/off devices.

1.8.5.en: Added cards for checking if a device is managed or capacity controlled

1.8.6.en: Just a number of smaller bugfixes.

1.9.0.en: PELS can now receive prices through a flow, for example from the Power by the Hour app.

1.10.0.en: Add support for Homey Energy as price provider.

1.11.0.en:
Daily Budget experience has been expanded and corrected: the Budget view now includes yesterday’s history, and several fixes improve how daily history is calculated and displayed. The Price view now contains prices with VAT, which makes it easier to compare prices to other sources.

The PELS Insights device now provides generated plan/price images (today and tomorrow) as camera tiles.

1.11.1.en: Fix a bug where images won't render on Homey due to Homey running on arm64

1.11.2.en: Increase performance by reducing plan generation churn.

1.12.0.en: Adds support for devices with only cumulating power meter capabilities. Adds support for aircon devices.

1.12.1.en: The app is now more responsive to real-time power changes: plan rebuilds can now be triggered by device power updates, so shedding/restoring reacts faster to actual load shifts. There’s also clearer, safer behavior around capacity control for individual devices. Devices are restored when capacity control is disabled, and uncontrolled devices won’t be toggled on unless they were actually shed.

1.13.0.en: Uncontrolled load will now have a stronger influence on the budget than controlled load. Both because PELS can then better predict the need for load from devices it doesn't control and that it avoids the budget becoming a self-fulfilling prophecy.

1.13.1.en: The Overview now gives clearer at-a-glance plan status with new state badges and price-style indicators, and several daily budget/price shaping details have been refined so planning better reflects real household load (including better handling of uncontrolled load and budget split edge cases). Under the hood, this release reduces planning churn and CPU spikes by tightening plan rebuild scheduling and throttling non-critical snapshot writes, while still keeping plan data fresh. Performance logging was also streamlined to focus on high-value metrics, making diagnostics easier and less noisy.

1.14.0.en: PELS now supports temperature-only devices (like VThermo) as managed devices for price optimization even when they have no power measurement or configured load; capacity control remains disabled for those devices. Limit-reason reporting is more accurate around restore cooldown/shed scenarios, and the price summary now shows cheap/expensive threshold caps.

1.14.1.en: Added support for Norgespris support scheme.

1.14.2.en: Added support for the airtreatment class including Flexit Nordic balanced ventilation units.

1.14.3.en: Fix restore loop for devices without onoff capability.

1.15.0.en:
PELS now reads expected device usage from Homey’s Energy settings on the device (Advanced Settings → Energy), instead of relying only on inferred defaults. This is the “Power usage when on/off” configuration in Homey. Using that source makes expected usage in PELS match the values users have actually configured per device.

Other fixes in this update are mainly around reliability and UI consistency: priority/order changes now trigger plan refresh more consistently, priority-only plan changes are reflected in the overview, and a single unavailable device no longer stops actions for other devices in the same loop. Debug logging around Homey device/energy data was also expanded for troubleshooting.

1.15.1.en: Aligned capacity shortfall behavior with hourly hard-cap budget semantics: shortfall now only triggers when PELS projects that the hourly hard cap will be breached and no controllable devices are left to shed. Updated related Flow/capability wording and Insights messages to match, removed timeline shortfall notifications, and included documentation plus dependency/security maintenance updates.

1.15.2.en: Fix shortfall checks during daily-limit shedding.

1.16.0.en:
This release refreshes the app’s UI and terminology to make PELS easier to understand and use. The settings pages have been polished across the Budget, Usage, and chart views with better mobile layouts, improved usage toggles and heatmap rendering, and more consistent visuals. The app and PELS Insights device also get updated icons, and several labels now use clearer wording such as headroom and current soft limit to better match what PELS is actually measuring and enforcing.

Daily Budget behavior has also been made more robust and predictable. PELS now uses observed hourly usage patterns to keep daily plans within more realistic bounds, improving pacing, restores, and price shaping while reducing edge-case errors around rollover, stale plans, and cap blending. Startup and chart rendering performance have also been tightened up, so the app feels more responsive while keeping the same core control model.

1.16.1.en: Fix packaging so that the built app has all runtime dependencies.

1.16.2.en:
Improved the Settings UI so live power data, stale-data status, and the Overview update more reliably without needing manual refresh.

Improved the “Is there headroom for device?” flow condition with built-in cooldown/hysteresis after step-downs and recent PELS control changes, making headroom checks more stable and reducing rapid re-ramping.

1.17.0.en: This release adds experimental support for EV chargers in PELS. When enabled in Advanced settings, PELS can now detect and control supported official Homey EV chargers, so EV charging can participate in capacity control as a managed load.

1.18.0.en: Adds new price-aware Flow cards that let you build automations when the current price is among today’s lowest hours, or among the lowest hours before a chosen time. Also improves load shedding and restore decisions for managed devices, especially for devices that use expected power or stepped power levels, and reduces unnecessary churn when using the Set expected power for device Flow card by ignoring unchanged values.

1.18.1.en: Prevent budget split recalculation of past hours. Various stability and maintainability improvements.

1.18.2.en:
The Daily Budget confidence indicator now uses recent usage and budget history instead of a simple sample-count estimate, so it better reflects how predictable the home’s consumption is and how closely usage follows the planned budget.

Also includes stability and performance improvements in the Daily Budget calculations.

1.19.0.en:
Added per-device diagnostics in the device details view. You can now see recent blocked time, shed and restore activity, activation instability, and penalty history over the last 1, 7, and 21 days to help spot devices that are frequently held back or oscillate.

PELS is also more conservative about reactivating devices that quickly end up being shed again. Devices that flap will now need more headroom before they are allowed back on, which reduces repeated on/off cycling while still keeping shedding fast when capacity is tight.

1.19.1.en: Fix a bug that prevented device diagnostics from loading.

1.19.2.en: Significant improvements of stability and performance.

1.20.0.en: Added flow cards to add or remove daily budget exemptions per device, plus a condition card to check exemption status. You can now open device details directly from the Overview tab. Also improves DST and price handling, and various performance improvements.

1.20.2.en: Improved drift detection and fixed a few bugs where the app did not respond to drift.

1.20.3.en: Improved settlements where PELS state and device state may be out of sync due to e.g cloud API latency.

1.20.4.en: Reduce image rendering memory footprint by replacing resvg with canvas.

1.20.5.en: Reduce memory footprint by imoprting only what we need from Homey SDK

1.20.6.en: Simplified device fetching/updating, resulting in reduced memory footprint.

1.20.7.en: Remove unused files from app archive.

2.0.0.en:
The PELS Insights device no longer provides generated plan and price camera images. If you used those images in dashboards or flows, the budget widget is now the intended replacement, and the related image-based Flow card has been deprecated.

This release also moves stepped-load devices closer to becoming first-class citizens in PELS. Support for stepped-load control has been expanded and refined so these devices fit better into planning and control, along with a number of smaller stability and UI improvements.

2.1.0.en: Add support for fetching whole-home power usage through Homey Energy.

2.1.1.en: Various bug fixes for stepped load.

2.1.2.en: Various bug fixes for stepped load.

2.1.6.en: Various bug fixes. Tightened shed action set step logic by ensuring it's the lowest non-zero step that is used.

2.1.8.en: Fixed a bug where devices may be shed while stepped devices are running at max.

2.1.9.en: Updated widget previews to conform with guidelines. Also includes a number of bug fixes.

2.1.10.en: Updated widget previews to conform with guidelines.

2.1.12.en: A larger set of bug fixes that reduce restoration/shed churn.

2.1.13.en: Reduce redundant work when using Homey Energy power sampling.
